title: × Echinostele cabrerae
intro: Echinostele cabrerae is a plant species, commonly known as the "Philippine Coral Cactus," that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Echinostele cabrerae include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections.
